    <p>{`The documentation lives in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`docs`}</inlineCode>{` folder.`}</p>
    <p>{`We build the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://ibm.github.io/spm-ui-upgrade-helper/"
      }}>{`documentation site`}</a>{` during the Travis build using Gatsby and we deploy it to Github Pages.`}</p>
    <p>{`The build copies the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`docs`}</inlineCode>{` folder to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`packages/gatsby-docs/src/pages`}</inlineCode>{`, makes some minor alternations to make it work with the Carbon theme, and deploys it. Specifically, it converts the level 1 headings into “title” attributes, and removes the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`[<< Back to previous page]`}</inlineCode>{` links.`}</p>
    <p>{`Doing this allows us to keep our documentation files in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`docs`}</inlineCode>{` folder in case we ever need to switch back to using standard Github pages (for example if there is an error in the Gatsby deploy.)`}</p>
    <p>{`Note that the Gatsby navigation sidebar is hand-crafted, so if you add a new page or move or rename an existing page you will have to update `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`packages/gatsby-docs/src/data/nav-items.yaml`}</inlineCode>{` to match.`}</p>
    <h2>{`Local development with Gatsby`}</h2>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`cd packages/gatsby-docs`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`yarn install`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`yarn gatsby:dev`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Open `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "http://localhost:8000"
        }}>{`http://localhost:8000`}</a></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Make changes and run `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`yarn convert-docs`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The page will hot-reload`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2>{`Local development without Gatsby`}</h2>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`View markdown files in VSCode`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`cmd + shift + v`}</inlineCode>{` to preview the files`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Pages will hot-reload on changes`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2>{`Gatsby and Travis`}</h2>
    <p>{`Gatsby will only be deployed if the build contains changes to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`docs`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`packages/gatsby-docs`}</inlineCode>{` folders. This is done to speed up the build.`}</p>
    <p>{`See `}<a parentName="p" {...{

    <h2>{`Switching between Gatsby and GitHub Pages`}</h2>
    <p>{`If you need to switch back to GitHub Pages, for example while you fix an error in the Gatsby deploy, you can do so by navigating to `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://github.com/IBM/spm-ui-upgrade-helper/settings/pages"
      }}>{`https://github.com/IBM/spm-ui-upgrade-helper/settings/pages`}</a>{` and changing the branch to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`main`}</inlineCode>{` and the folder to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`/docs`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`To change back to Gatsby, set the branch to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`gh-pages`}</inlineCode>{` and the folder to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`/`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
